Description:
Our IASAS Drama Showcase was truly unforgettable—an incredible part of the IASAS Dance, Drama & Tech Cultural Conventions (DDT)!
The students gave a stunning performance of Naomi Iizuka's Anon(ymous), bringing powerful stories to life with raw emotion, compelling storytelling, and outstanding stage presence. Their dedication and passion were evident in every moment!
Now, they’re headed to Singapore (March 5-9) to represent us at the  IASAS Dance, Drama & Tech Cultural Conventions (DDT)! Let’s send them all our best wishes as they share their incredible talent on the international stage! 🌍🎬
#IASAS2025 #DramaMagic #ISB #ProudSchool #DramaOnStage #IASASDDT #ActingExcellence #DramaShowcase #IASAS #CulturalConventions

This year, ISB turns 75.

Seventy-five years of students walking through these gates.
Seventy-five years of teachers opening doors.
Seventy-five years of families building community in Bangkok.

It began with a small group of internationally mobile families who believed a school could do more than deliver lessons.

Over time, that belief shaped something special.

Friendships that span continents.
Teachers who change trajectories.
Students who grow into leaders, creators, and global citizens.

On May 14, we will mark 75 years.

Between now and then, we’ll be sharing stories — moments, memories, milestones — that shaped ISB into the community it is today.

If you’ve been part of ISB’s journey, tell us your first ISB memory in the comments.

#ISB75

Thank you to our amazing parent community for making the Chinese Food Fair such a wonderful success! From authentic dishes to refreshing drinks, the event brought our school community together to celebrate culture, connection, and delicious food. We truly appreciate all the parent volunteers who made this memorable event possible.

Thank you to our Grade 10 and 11 students and parents for joining us last night for U.S. University Case Studies.

Together, we explored what truly shapes the college application experience, from the hidden workload to the importance of building a thoughtful and balanced college list. We are proud to see our families approaching this process with greater clarity, perspective, and confidence.

This was a highly competitive event featuring 70 teams from across Thailand, showcasing an inspiring level of innovation, engineering, and collaboration.
All eight of our teams earned team awards, including trophies in Robot Performance and CORE Values, recognizing their teamwork, creativity, and problem-solving. Two of our teams placed in the top 11 out of 70 teams in the Robot Game — an outstanding national achievement!
Beyond the awards, what stood out most was the incredible growth of our students. They:
✨ Persevered through technical challenges
✨ Presented their innovation projects confidently to judges
✨ Collaborated under pressure
✨ Represented ISB with integrity and enthusiasm
Most importantly, they had fun celebrating six months of learning, design, coding, and teamwork.
A heartfelt thank you to our dedicated Panther Robotics coaching team for their countless hours of support:
Mr. Marshall, Ms. Pool, Ms. Marshall, Mr. Przybylski, and Mr. Ciambrello.
We are so proud of our Panthers! 🐾💛

A huge thank you to all 82 donors who joined our Blood Drive this week. Our Panthers donated 69 units of blood to the Thai Red Cross. See you again next Fall, and thank you to alt.Eatery for the refreshments.

The 18 members of ISB's IASAS Speech and Debate team displayed their talents at our recent Speech and Debate Showcase. They will be heading off to Jakarta Intercultural School on Wednesday, Feb. 25th to compete against the best speakers from the other IASAS Schools in the disciplines of Original Oratory, Oral Interpretation, Impromptu Speaking, Extemporaneous Speaking and Debate.  Expectations are high after ISB's unprecedented return of 4 gold and 1 silver medal at last year's event in Taipei.

IFF wouldn’t have been possible without our amazing community. Thank you to all the organizers, performers, volunteers, and everyone who joined us to celebrate together. Your support and spirit made this event unforgettable. We can’t wait to do it all again. See you next year! 💛

What an incredible night at the New ISB Theater. Huge thanks to everyone who joined us for Faculty Follies last night.

Our faculty and staff traded their classrooms for the stage to bring the community a night of comedy, choreography, and even some enthusiastic Canadian sea shanties. It was a wonderful evening of laughter and lighthearted fun, all while showing off a completely different side of our talented ISB team.

Most importantly, all proceeds from the ticket sales go directly to support iCare. Thank you for showing up, laughing with us, and contributing to such a great cause.

We are proud to be part of such a vibrant and supportive community.

Thank you for joining Junior University Kick Off (JUKO) last night, hosted by HS University Counseling — a wonderful start to planning the years ahead.

A huge congratulations to Team Brick Malfunction (Gr. 5) for taking 1st place overall with 375 points! 🏆 And in a thrilling finish, Middle School narrowly edged out Elementary School to reclaim the coveted ISB Team Trophy.

Special shoutout to our rookie Grade 4 team, Bouncy Powered Diamonds, for earning the CORE Values Award! 💛

We’re so proud of all our teams—this was fantastic practice as they gear up for the Thailand National Tournament on Feb 14–15. Go Panthers! 🐾💛🖤

For the first time ever, our 𝗜𝗔𝗦𝗔𝗦 𝗚𝗼𝗹𝗱 𝗶𝗻 𝗦𝗼𝗰𝗰𝗲𝗿, 𝗖𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗖𝗼𝘂𝗻𝘁𝗿𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗩𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝘆𝗯𝗮𝗹𝗹 - 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗮𝗺𝗲 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿.Three teams. Three trophies. One unstoppable force.
These athletes showed what it means to be an ISB Panther - taking risks, pushing limits, and proving that girls’ sports aren’t just part of the story…  𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘆 𝗔𝗥𝗘 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲.
To every athlete, coach, and supporter: thank you for lifting each other up and showing our community what unity, grit, and girl power look like at ISB.

On 12 November, our ISB community gathered to honor Her Majesty Queen Sirikit The Queen Mother. Students, families, and guests - including H.E. Ms. Supamas Isarabhakdi from the Prime Minister’s Office - came together to celebrate her legacy of culture, artistry, and service.
From the student-led Royal Anthem to the Khon performance, poetry, reflections, the commemorative film, and the ROTC Tribute, each moment connected our international community to the beauty and depth of Thai heritage.
Our Royal Exhibition invited students to explore culture together through traditional dress, biography, and hands-on weaving and mask-making. These shared experiences help our learners grow as global citizens, curious, respectful, and ready to contribute in an interconnected world.
#ISB #InternationalSchoolBangkok #ThaiCulture #QueenMother #ISBCommunity #HonoringTradition

Grade 8 and finance-interested high school students were inspired by Khun Jirayut “Topp” Srupsrisopa, Founder and Group CEO of Bitkub Capital Group Holdings. This event was co-hosted by both the middle school and the high school “Next Gen” Financial Literacy Club, a group dedicated to empowering our generation and the next with essential financial literacy skills.
Our mission was a wonderful success. Students gained essential investment knowledge and left feeling motivated to take charge of their financial futures.
We are deeply grateful to Khun Topp for dedicating his valuable time to inspire our students and for his generous offer to continue this momentum through future collaborations with Bitkub, especially for our High School students.

This week, the ISB community came together in deep respect and remembrance of Her Majesty Queen Sirikit, The Queen Mother of Thailand, who passed away peacefully on 24 October 2025 at the age of 93.
In separate assemblies across the Elementary, Middle, and High Schools, students, faculty, and staff paid tribute to Her Majesty’s lifelong devotion to the nation. Her compassion, leadership, and dedication to the Thai people continue to inspire us all.
May Her Majesty Queen Sirikit, The Queen Mother, rest in eternal peace, and may her legacy live on in our hearts.

#ISB #ISBTHAILAND

International School Bangkok (ISB) joins the people of Thailand in mourning the passing of Her Majesty Queen Sirikit, The Queen Mother.  Her grace, love, and devotion to the nation will forever be remembered with heartfelt sorrow and deepest respect.

This week’s ISB MUN Showcase Debate tackled a timely and thought-provoking topic: the threat of bioterrorism amidst the rising accessibility of genome editing. The quality of speeches was outstanding — both in delivery and in depth of content.

Delegates, impeccably turned out in formal wear, carried themselves with the poise and professionalism of true diplomats.

In his Chair’s Report preceding the debate, ISB MUN Secretary General, Charlie Doherty, urged delegates to engage deeply with the issue, reminding them that “it is only by fostering collaboration between the scientific community, academia, individuals, public and private stakeholders, and the international community that we can come closer to utilizing the double-edged sword of biotechnology primarily for the benefit of humanity.”

The debate certainly embodied this spirit of collaboration — showcasing critical thinking, eloquence, and a shared commitment to ensuring biotechnology serves the progress, not the peril, of humankind.

#ISB #ISBThailand #ISBMUN #ISBHighSchool

MS Wai Kru Assembly 2025
On September 5, 2025, MS students and teachers gathered in the ISB Theatre for the Wai Kru Assembly, a cherished Thai tradition that honors teachers with gratitude and respect. The program featured the King’s Anthem, student MCs, the traditional teacher worship chant, floral garland presentations to new faculty, administrators, counselors, and instructional assistants, as well as student-created media expressing gratitude and a performance by the MS Choir.

This cultural assembly also encouraged students to be ready to learn for the new academic year, highlighting ISB values of Responsibility and Commitment. Teachers shared tips for success, while the program also brought plenty of energy and fun. Students enjoyed a lively hand-clap brain break and Ready to Learn: House Charades, where each House teamed up with their teachers on stage to act and guess words—building teamwork, integrity, and school spirit. The program closed with Principal Mr. Harter’s inspiring words on gratitude and the joy of learning.

The Wai Kru Assembly was a wonderful success, celebrating Thai culture, ISB values, and the strong bond between students and teachers. Well done, Middle School!

Confidence was high in our boys varsity golf team this season as we geared up for IASAS in Kuala Lumpur. But these things are never easy—we lost one of our strongest players to illness the day before we travelled.
Strong performances in rounds one and two secured us a spot in the final against a tough TAS team. In a closely fought battle, four of the six matches went to the final hole, but our boys held their nerve and came out on top—clinching our third consecutive gold medal! 
Special shout-out to Kevin Xia, who made birdie on his final hole in round two to win his second consecutive individual gold!
So proud of all our players for showing incredible grit, skill, and staying calm under pressure.

A big thank you to everyone who joined us last Friday for the opening of the Elementary Art Show at the new ISB Gallery: CHEE VIT CHEE VA! 🖌️💫
This vibrant exhibition showcases our young artists' interpretations of life in Thailand, celebrating the beauty, culture, and everyday moments that make this country so special. If you missed the opening, the show is still running until March 19th—don’t miss your chance to experience it!
